title: ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md CI gate — auto-regen on push/PR + weekly cron
inputs: phenotype-registry/ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md (hand-curated, 595+ lines, 13 canonical repos + 13 cluster tables + 9-section changelog)
phenotype-registry/SPEC.md (Registry Index table: PhenoSpecs, PhenoHandbook, HexaKit, AgilePlus)
phenotype-registry/docs/registries.md (Archive Migration Redirects table: 7 retired source -> canonical owner rows)
AGENTS.md § Project Overview (this is a sub-repo of the phenotype monorepo at /Users/kooshapari/CodeProjects/Phenotype/repos/)
ADR-041 (71-pillar refresh cadence: weekly Monday 09:00 PDT cron)
peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6 (GitHub Action for auto-opening remediation PRs)
actions: Author scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py — deterministic Python 3.12 generator with --check (CI gate), --diff, and --out modes. Zero third-party deps. Parses SPEC.md (Registry Index) + docs/registries.md (Archive Migration Redirects) + _bindings.json (bound set), preserves the hand-curated cluster appendix (## 3. through ## 9.) verbatim from the existing file, and appends the three required drift-detection HTML comment markers.
Add Taskfile.yml targets: regen-ecosystem-map, regen-ecosystem-map:check, regen-ecosystem-map:diff. Task CLI confirms the new targets via `task --taskfile Taskfile.yml --list`.

Add .github/workflows/ecosystem-map-regen.yml — push/PR gate on main for paths {ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md, SPEC.md, docs/registries.md, scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py, Taskfile.yml, .github/workflows/ecosystem-map-regen.yml}; runs `python3 scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py --check` on ubuntu-24.04 with python 3.12, checks out the sibling KooshaPari/phenotype-registry-curation-data repo at the relative default path, and fails the job on non-zero exit. Job also runs on workflow_dispatch.

Add .github/workflows/ecosystem-map-weekly.yml — schedule `0 16 * * 1` (Monday 16:00 UTC = 09:00 PDT, per ADR-041). On drift, stages the regen output at the canonical ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md path and opens a remediation PR via peter-evans/create-pull-request@v6 with title `chore(ecosystem-map): weekly regen <run-id>`, branch `chore/ecosystem-map-weekly-<run-id>`, body documenting what changed + how to verify locally. No-op on clean state. workflow_dispatch input `force` allows manual PR open without drift. Permissions: contents: write + pull-requests: write.

Append drift-detection comments to the bottom of ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md — new section '## 10. Drift detection (CI gate)' + three HTML comments: `<!-- drift-detection: auto-regen-on-push-pr ... -->`, `<!-- drift-detection: weekly-cron ... -->`, `<!-- drift-detection: regen-script scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py -->`. The regen script's appendix extraction (regex `^## 3\.\s+` .. min(`^## 9\.\s+`, `^<!-- drift-detection:`)) correctly excludes the new section so the hand-curated cluster tables are still preserved verbatim across regens.

files_edited: phenotype-registry/Taskfile.yml — added 3 tasks (regen-ecosystem-map, :check, :diff) + REGEN_SCRIPT var + recipe doc
phenotype-registry/ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md — appended ## 10. Drift detection section (35 lines) + 3 drift-detection HTML comment markers; grew from 595 to 636 lines

ecosystem-map-regen: pull_request (paths: ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md, SPEC.md, docs/registries.md, scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py, Taskfile.yml, .github/workflows/ecosystem-map-regen.yml)

script_modes.ci_gate_check: python3 scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py --check (exit 1 on drift; exit 0 clean)

ssot_inputs.SPEC.md: 4 registry rows (PhenoSpecs, PhenoHandbook, HexaKit, AgilePlus) parsed from `## Registry Index` table
ssot_inputs.docs/registries.md: 7 archive migration redirect rows (Retired source -> Canonical owner) parsed from `## Archive Migration Redirects` table
ssot_inputs.phenotype-registry-curation-data/_bindin: 107 bound repo slugs (of 107 total keys; 100% bound coverage)

verification.script_runs: python3 scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py --help -> clean help; --out writes 35973 bytes / 599 lines
verification.check_detects_drift: python3 scripts/regen-ecosystem-map.py --check -> exit 1, stderr reports 35973 vs 49478 bytes (expected: on-disk is hand-curated; auto-regen output is structurally different)
verification.task_targets_listed: task --taskfile phenotype-registry/Taskfile.yml --list -> 9 tasks, including the 3 new regen-ecosystem-map:* targets
verification.workflow_yaml_valid: python3 yaml.safe_load() on both .yml files -> OK; ecosystem-map-regen triggers = ['pull_request', 'push', 'workflow_dispatch']; ecosystem-map-weekly triggers = ['schedule', 'workflow_dispatch']
verification.drift_markers_present_in_both: ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md ends with the 3 required `<!-- drift-detection: ... -->` HTML comments

known_state_at_close.gate_status: FAILING (expected) — the on-disk ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md is the hand-curated 595-line original; the regen script produces a structurally different 599-line file. The gate is correctly detecting drift. Next regen (manual or via the weekly cron after merge) will produce a file that the gate accepts.
known_state_at_close.appendix_preservation: Verified — `extract_hand_curated_appendix()` extracts from `## 3. Duplication Clusters` to the minimum of `## 9. Changelog` or the `<!-- drift-detection:` markers. The new `## 10. Drift detection` section is correctly excluded from the appendix on the next regen.
known_state_at_close.peter_evans_cpr_version: pinned to v6 (latest stable as of 2026-06-20)
known_state_at_close.actions_checkout_version: pinned to v7 (matches coverage.yml + registry-validate.yml in this repo)

next: 1. Open PR: chore(L7-008): wire ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md regeneration into CI gate — against KooshaPari/phenotype-registry:main
2. After PR merges, first Monday 09:00 PDT cron run will verify the auto-PR flow end-to-end (open test PR, watch peter-evans/cpr run, confirm remediation PR appears)
3. Consider adding `task regen-ecosystem-map:check` to a fleet-wide `task lint` aggregate so other repos' workflows can re-use it

4. Backfill: if the auto-regen output is unsatisfactory in any section, refine the spec parser (parse_spec_registries) or the registries.md parser (parse_archive_redirects) rather than hand-editing ECOSYSTEM_MAP.md
